query_string: Ecclesiastical History of the English People

tossups.question: This work describes how the Dalreudians conquered a land inhabited by an ethnicity ruled by a king “from the female royal race.” This work’s preface declares Ceolwulf (“KAY-uhl-wulf”) as the “most glorious king” and thanks Albinus for assistance on it. This work details such events as the martyrdom of St. Alban and naming of (*) Augustine as the first Archbishop of Canterbury. For 10 points, name this work by the Venerable Bede that chronicles the advent of Christianity in England.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Ecclesiastical History of the English People
tossups.question_sanitized: This work describes how the Dalreudians conquered a land inhabited by an ethnicity ruled by a king "from the female royal race." This work's preface declares Ceolwulf ("KAY-uhl-wulf") as the "most glorious king" and thanks Albinus for assistance on it. This work details such events as the martyrdom of St. Alban and naming of (*) Augustine as the first Archbishop of Canterbury. For 10 points, name this work by the Venerable Bede that chronicles the advent of Christianity in England.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about monks who wrote British history in the early Middle Ages, for 10 points each.
parts: The Venerable Bede is best remembered for this five-book work completed around 731 CE that praises Edwin of Northumbria as the ideal Christian king.

This Welsh monk from St. David’s Abbey is best known for writing a Life of King Alfred the Great in 893 CE, after he was invited to Alfred’s court and given the monasteries of Congresbury and Banwell.

This Celtic monk from Rhuys is a key source for Britain during the 5th and 6th centuries. He blasts rulers like the tyrant king Vortigern for inviting in the Saxons and sealing the doom of the nation in his work De Excidio Britanniae, or On the Ruin of Britain.
answers: The Ecclesiastical History of the English People [or Historia Ecclesiastica Gentis Anglorum or History of the English Church]
Gildas [or St. Gildas Bandonicus or Gildas the Wise or Gildas Sapiens]
answers_sanitized: The Ecclesiastical History of the English People [or Historia Ecclesiastica Gentis Anglorum or History of the English Church]
Gildas [or St. Gildas Bandonicus or Gildas the Wise or Gildas Sapiens]
